questionText: For your electricity generation activities, provide details of your nameplate capacity and electricity generation specifics for each technology employed.
questionKo: 귀사의 전력 발전 활동에 대해 사용 기술별 설비용량과 전력 생산 세부 정보를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Each power generation source is associated with specific dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities, requiring tailored management procedures and future planning. For example, hydropower will use and impact water resources in a different way to biomass power generation plants. Asking organizations to disclose their power generation sources provides data users with the contextual information necessary to understand and evaluate how they assess and manage climate- and water-related dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities. This also enables data users to make more accurate cross-organizational and cross-industry comparisons.

rows.title: Fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture and storage

columns.text: Own or control operations which use this power generation source
columns.textKo: 또는 통제 운영 사용 이 배출원

guidance.textKo: 전년 대비 변경사항은 2026 CDP 원문 및 변경 추적표 기준으로 확인하십시오.

guidance.text: This question only appears if ‘Electricity generation' is selected in 1.16.
guidance.textKo: 이 문항 만 표시됨 만약 ‘전력 발전' 선택됨 1.16.

guidance.text:
General 
Report nameplate capacity by primary power generation sources owned or controlled by your organization.
Report your preferred measure (gross or net) of generation. Although CDP encourages disclosure of both, reporting one measure is sufficient.
Refer to the CDP Technical note on Biofuels for guidance on biomass/biofuel sustainability.
Power generation source (column 0) 
See guidance for specific power generation sources below. The examples are illustrative and are not meant to be exhaustive:
"Coal - Hard": report coal generation using anthracite and/or bituminous coal.
"Coal - Brown": report coal generation using sub-bituminous coal and/or lignite.
"Sustainable biomass" and
"Other biomass" : biomass may be combusted on its own or co-fired with other fuels. Provide aggregated data for the biomass that you combust on its own and biomass that you co-combust with other fuel sources. 
"Waste (non-biomass)" : waste can include tire-derived fuels and other refuse-derived fuels. When reporting in this category, only report the nameplate capacity and gross electricity generation data from the non-biomass fraction. The biomass fraction should be reported under either biomass option. 
"Hydropower" : hydropower does not include pumped storage operations for the reporting of nameplate capacity and gross electricity generation. CDP regards it as a form of managing or storing energy rather than primary generation. Note you will be asked to report water accounting volumes for these operations. 
"
Wave ": wave energy can include technologies such as point absorbers, hinge rafts/attenuators, oscillating wave surge converters or water columns.
"
Tidal ": tidal technology can include energy generation from rise and fall tides, ebb and flood currents, as well as the use of horizontal or vertical-axis turbines.
If you are using hydrogen for power generation:
Report generation using renewably derived hydrogen in row 
"Other renewable". 
Report generation using hydrogen derived from fossil fuels in row 
"Other non-renewable". 
Report generation using hydrogen produced from fossil fuels using carbon capture and storage technology (i.e. blue hydrogen) in row 
"Fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture and storage" .
In all cases, provide the nameplate capacity and generation figures associated with this in the Comment field.
If part of your organization's power plant capacity is composed of multiple mixed small-scale generation technologies that are difficult to report by specific power generation source, then these can be aggregated by renewable and non-renewable sources. Report the total capacity of aggregated renewable sources under "Other renewable" and the total capacity of aggregated non-renewable sources under "Other non-renewable". You can explain the methodology utilized and list the small-scale technologies used in the comment section. 
Comment (column 5) (optional) 
Use this column to provide any additional relevant information.
If you report information in the "Sustainable biomass" row, provide the criteria used to classify the biomass as sustainable (e.g. certification).
If you report information in any of the biomass rows, specify if the biomass technology refers to bioenergy plants fitted with carbon capture and storage (BECCS).
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 해당 문항에서 CDP가 요청하는 작성 내용을 설명합니다. 위 응답방법, 열 구조, 드롭다운 선택지를 기준으로 보고연도와 조직 범위에 맞는 정보를 입력하십시오.

guidance.text:
Biomass: any organic matter, i.e. biological material, available on a renewable basis. Includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and industrial sources. Biomass fuels should be sustainably sourced and certified where possible, and include: 
Solid biofuels: solid fuels derived from biomass. Includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and industrial sources. 
Biogas: a mixture of methane (CH4) and carbon dioxide (CO2) used as fuel and produced by bacterial degradation of organic matter or through gasification of biomass. 
Liquid biofuels: liquid fuels derived from biomass such as ethanol and biodiesel. 
Carbon capture and storage (CCS): as defined by the IEA, a family of technologies and techniques that enable the capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) from fuel combustion or industrial processes, the transport of CO2 via shops or pipelines, and its storage underground, in depleted oil and gas fields and deep saline formations. 
Gross electricity generation: the total amount of electricity produced by a power generation plant before accounting for any internal consumption or losses within the plant itself. It is typically measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). 
Nameplate capacity: the maximum rated output of a generator, prime mover, or other electric power production equipment under specific conditions designated by the manufacturer. It is typically measured in megawatts (MW). 
Net electricity generation: the total electricity output from a power generation plant available for external use or distribution after deducting the electricity consumed internally for the plant's own operational needs. It is typically measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). 
Renewable energy : energy taken from the sources that are inexhaustible, e.g. wind, water, solar, geothermal energy and sustainable biofuels (adapted from GHG Protocol, 2004 ).
guidance.textKo: 이 섹션은 문항에서 사용하는 주요 용어의 의미를 설명합니다. 용어 해석은 CDP 2026 원문과 국문 양식의 용어 사용을 우선 적용하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed (excluding both rows 'Other renewable' AND 'Other non-renewable'). Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
Either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR column 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' is completed in addition to all other columns.
A maximum of 8.5/8.5 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 이 평가기준은 해당 점수 수준에서 요구되는 조건입니다. 배점표, 문항 응답 구조, 완료 셀, 선택지 및 설명 입력 여부를 함께 확인하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Full Disclosure points must be awarded to be eligible for Awareness points.
i) Figure in row 'Total' in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' excluding row 'Total'
OR
Figure in row 'Total' in column 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in column 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' excluding row 'Total' - 2 points
ii) Figure in row 'Total' in column 'Nameplate capacity (MW)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in column 'Nameplate capacity (MW)' excluding row 'Total' - 2 points
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 이 평가기준은 해당 점수 수준에서 요구되는 조건입니다. 배점표, 문항 응답 구조, 완료 셀, 선택지 및 설명 입력 여부를 함께 확인하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Full Awareness points must be awarded to be eligible for Management points.
ROUTE A)
Sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- 'Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- 'Onshore wind'
- 'Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- 'Wave'
- 'Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
...accounts for greater than 25% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 2 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- 'Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- 'Onshore wind'
- 'Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- 'Wave'
- 'Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
... accounts for between 10% and 25% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 1 point
A maximum of 1/2 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 이 평가기준은 해당 점수 수준에서 요구되는 조건입니다. 배점표, 문항 응답 구조, 완료 셀, 선택지 및 설명 입력 여부를 함께 확인하십시오.

criteria.criteria:
Full Management points must be awarded to be eligible for Leadership points.
ROUTE A)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- ‘Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- ‘Onshore wind'
- ‘Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- ‘Wave'
- ‘Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
...accounts for greater than 90% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 3 points.
A maximum of 3/3 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- ‘Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- ‘Onshore wind'
- ‘Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- ‘Wave'
- ‘Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
...accounts for greater than 75% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 2/3 points.
A maximum of 2/3 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE C)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- ‘Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- ‘Onshore wind'
- ‘Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- ‘Wave'
- ‘Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
...accounts for greater than 50% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh) - 1/3 points.
A maximum of 1/3 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 이 평가기준은 해당 점수 수준에서 요구되는 조건입니다. 배점표, 문항 응답 구조, 완료 셀, 선택지 및 설명 입력 여부를 함께 확인하십시오.

change.question_ko: 귀사의 전력 발전 활동에 대해 사용 기술별 설비용량과 전력 생산 세부 정보를 입력하십시오.
change.q2025_text: For your electricity generation activities, provide details of your nameplate capacity and electricity generation specifics for each technology employed.
change.change_summary: Rows "Ocean thermal"; "Wave"; "Tidal" added. Row "Wind" replaced with "Onshore wind and "Offshore wind". Row "Lignite" replaced with "Coal-brown". Additional guidance added to clarify the reporting of blue hydrogen.
change.scoring_change_summary:
CC/W Energy utilities and power generators Disclosure • Point allocation increased from 7.5 to 8.5 to reflect additional power generation sources added to question; ‘Ocean thermal', ‘Onshore wind', ‘Offshore wind', ‘Wave & Tidal' are newly added while ‘Wind' & ‘Marine' have been removed. Update in accordance with questionnaire change.
CC Energy utilities and power generators Management • ROUTE A updated to reflect additional power generation sources added to question; ‘Ocean thermal', ‘Onshore wind', ‘Offshore wind', ‘Wave & Tidal' are newly added while ‘Wind' & ‘Marine' have been removed. Update in accordance with questionnaire change.
CC Energy utilities and power generators Leadership • ROUTE A updated to reflect additional power generation sources added to question; ‘Ocean thermal', ‘Onshore wind', ‘Offshore wind', ‘Wave & Tidal' are newly added while ‘Wind' & ‘Marine' have been removed. Update in accordance with questionnaire change.
change.daml_summary: Energy utilities & power generators: D / | A / | M / | L /

simpleHelpKo: 이 문항은 '전력, 제공 세부 정보 전력.'에 대한 배경 설명입니다. CDP 응답자는 보고연도 기준으로 조직의 환경 관련 현황, 리스크, 기회, 성과 또는 관리체계를 문항의 응답 구조에 맞춰 설명해야 합니다.
